author: y4rr
content:
Pass 5.B.4 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOW-UPS.

Source-doc reassessment caught audit's 'silently downgraded' framing as wrong: Tensor::data() at ferrotorch-core/src/tensor.rs:635-638 already Errs on CUDA with GpuTensorNotAccessible. The bug was doc drift + a generic error message, not silent demote.

Fix:
- fusion.rs:1103-1109 — explicit is_cuda() guard returns Err(NotImplementedOnCuda { op: 'apply_fused (FusedChain PTX runtime executor not yet wired; tracked in #1138)' }) instead of the implicit generic GpuTensorNotAccessible
- fusion.rs:1081-1101 — apply_fused doc rewritten to accurately describe CPU-only behavior + #1138 tracker reference; no more 'same device' / 'could be dispatched' aspirational text
- fusion.rs:1-13 — module doc tightened to match reality
- 2 new discriminating tests:
  - apply_fused_doc_does_not_promise_same_device (Linux-runnable doc-drift guard via include_str! reflection)
  - apply_fused_errs_on_cuda_input_with_clear_message (cfg-gated to CUDA runtime; honest gap on Linux/WSL2)

codegen.rs:987-1017 left untouched — STALE-NO-OP (already correctly fail-fast with JitError::GpuBackendUnavailable for GpuCuda/GpuPtx targets).

Verification: 311/311 lib + 232 integration tests pass; build + clippy -D warnings clean. Sabotage probe (doc mutation → 'same device' restored) captured verbatim panic; revert green. Cargo.toml untouched (no ferrotorch-gpu dep added — would be architectural unilateralism).

Follow-up #1138 filed for the workspace-level PTX runtime executor wiring (audit's Pattern B path; estimated ~500 LoC + new dep + test infra; cites ferrotorch-gpu/src/module_cache.rs:74 as PTX loader pattern).
